introSection:
Little Dipper is a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Conneaut Lake Park in Conneaut Lake, Pennsylvania, USA. It was built by the Allan Herschell Company, and began operating in 1950. In 2007 the ride closed for unknown reasons, and it reopened again in 2008. It was last known to have operated in 2021. After standing out of operation for several years, the ride returned to operation in 2025 for the park's Fall Pumpkin Fest event, following refurbishment and repainting.

It is the oldest operating steel coaster in the United States, and the third oldest operating steel coaster overall.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride is a standard Little Dipper model, which is designed for young children. It has a simple oval layout, with a single bunny-hill.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: It has been operating with the same train since 1950.

fullText:
Little Dipper is a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Conneaut Lake Park in Conneaut Lake, Pennsylvania, USA. It was built by the Allan Herschell Company, and began operating in 1950. In 2007 the ride closed for unknown reasons, and it reopened again in 2008. It was last known to have operated in 2021. After standing out of operation for several years, the ride returned to operation in 2025 for the park's Fall Pumpkin Fest event, following refurbishment and repainting.

It is the oldest operating steel coaster in the United States, and the third oldest operating steel coaster overall.

The ride is a standard Little Dipper model, which is designed for young children. It has a simple oval layout, with a single bunny-hill.

It has been operating with the same train since 1950.
